name: The Office (US)
description: Dunder Mifflin, Scranton, and the antics of Michael Scott's branch

questions.q: Who plays the Regional Manager Michael Scott in the U.S. version of 'The Office'?

source.notes: Carell played Michael for seasons 1-7 (2005-2011). Based on Gervais's David Brent.

questions.q: Who plays the receptionist-turned-salesperson Pam Beesly?

source.notes: Fischer had just one prior major TV role before landing Pam.

questions.q: Who plays Assistant (to the) Regional Manager Dwight Schrute?

source.notes: Dwight insists his title is 'Assistant Regional Manager' but Michael corrects him.

questions.q: What crop does Dwight Schrute grow on his family farm?

source.notes: Schrute Farms: 'Where the beets are beautiful and the silo is haunted.'

questions.q: Michael Scott famously drives which Chrysler model throughout the series?

source.notes: A convertible. Michael frequently bragged about its 'convertible-ness.'

questions.q: What is the full name of the fictional paper company at the center of 'The Office'?

source.notes: Dunder Mifflin Paper Company, Inc. The Scranton branch is the focus.

questions.q: What is the name of Angela Martin's beloved main cat?

source.notes: Dwight euthanizes Sprinkles in a freezer when Angela falls ill, thinking it merciful.

questions.q: What affectionate nickname does Andy Bernard give Jim Halpert?

source.notes: Given on Andy's first day when Jim eats a tuna sandwich.

questions.q: Phyllis's husband Bob Vance is the owner of what business?

source.notes: Bob always introduces himself as 'Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration.'

questions.q: What job does Ryan Howard start as at Dunder Mifflin?

source.notes: Played by B.J. Novak, who was also a writer and producer on the show.

questions.q: Which character works in HR and is frequently the target of Michael's disdain?

source.notes: Toby Flenderson, played by Paul Lieberstein (also a show writer and executive producer).

questions.q: What is Michael Scott's online dating username, revealed in the episode 'The Chump'?

source.notes: Michael insists it means he loves being a kid at heart.

questions.q: What is Jim and Pam's wedding destination in the series?

source.notes: Season 6 episode features the 'JK Wedding Entrance' spoof.

questions.q: What is the last name of Creed the eccentric employee, which the actor shares?

source.notes: Creed Bratton was a real member of The Grass Roots before acting.

questions.q: In 'The Injury,' Michael injures himself in what bizarre way?
choices: Burns his foot on a George Foreman Grill
Hits his head on a filing cabinet

source.notes: He grilled bacon on it in the morning from bed.

questions.q: What is the name of Michael Scott's amateur screenplay and its film-within-a-show?

source.notes: Written over 11 years. Features Jim as 'Goldenface.'

questions.q: In the iconic cold open, what prank does Jim pull on Dwight using Jell-O?

source.notes: Pilot episode cold open. Jim's very first prank on Dwight.

questions.q: In the season 5 episode 'Stress Relief,' Stanley has what medical event triggered by Dwight's fire safety demonstration?

source.notes: Dwight stages an elaborate fire alarm scenario, causing chaos.

questions.q: Andy Bernard is an alumnus of which Ivy League university, a fact he mentions constantly?

source.notes: He was an a cappella singer in 'Here Comes Treble.'

questions.q: What is the name of the fake currency Dwight invents to reward employees for good behavior?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced malformed yearbook-color question (no canonical Office moment). Dwight introduces Schrute Bucks in season 4 episode 'The Job'; he claims they can be exchanged for an extra five minutes of break time. 1 Schrute Buck = 1/100 of a cent.
